claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 16 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child, so let me start with some basic information.

**First question: Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 16 years old and lives with me at home. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 16 years old and lives with you. Let me ask you a few questions to determine eligibility.

Since Alex is 16, I need to check if he meets the education or training requirements. 

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** This would include things like:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar)
- Approved training programs
- Courses at school or college

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He left school and hasn't started anything else yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, I need to ask:

**Did Alex recently leave education or an approved training program?** If so, when did he leave?

This is important because there may be a temporary extension period where you could still claim Child Benefit after a young person leaves education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He left a few months ago, nothing really recent. He's just been at home since then, not really doing much to be honest.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To clarify - you mentioned he left "a few months ago." Could you tell me approximately how many months ago? 

The reason I'm asking is that there's a 20-week extension period after a young person leaves education during which you may still be eligible for Child Benefit, but only up until their 18th birthday. Since Alex is 16, I need to know if he left within the last 20 weeks (approximately 5 months) to see if this extension applies.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, it's been longer than that - probably about 6 or 7 months now, I'd say. So yeah, more than 5 months definitely.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me explain the situation regarding Child Benefit for Alex.

**Based on what you've told me, unfortunately you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.**

Here's why:

- Alex is 16 years old, which means he's over the automatic qualification age of under 16
- For young people aged 16-19, they must be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit
- Alex is not currently in education or training
- The 20-week extension period after leaving education has expired (it's been 6-7 months since he left)

**What this means:**

Child Benefit eligibility for 16-19 year olds requires them to be enrolled in approved full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels or similar qualifications) or approved training programs.

**What you could consider:**

If Alex decides to return to education or start an approved training program in the future, you may become eligible for Child Benefit again at that point. This would include things like:
- A-levels, T-levels, or similar qualifications
- NVQ levels 1-3
- BTEC courses (levels 1-3)
- Other approved non-advanced education or training

Would you like any clarification about these rules or what types of education or training would make Alex eligible?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. That's a shame, but I understand. So if he does decide to go back to college or something like that in the future, I could claim it then?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Yes, that's exactly right! If Alex enrolls in an approved education or training program in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for him again.

**What would qualify:**

The education or training would need to be:
- **Full-time** (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)
- **Non-advanced education**, such as:
  - A-levels or AS-levels
  - T-levels
  - BTECs (levels 1-3)
  - NVQs (levels 1-3)
  - Other similar college courses

**Important points to remember:**

- You can claim Child Benefit for him while he's in approved education up until his 20th birthday
- Advanced courses like university degrees or Higher National Certificates/Diplomas (HNC/HND) don't qualify
- If he starts a course, you should make your claim as soon as possible, as you can only backdate claims for up to 3 months

So if Alex does decide to go back to college or start a training program, that would be a good time to submit a new Child Benefit claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's all clear now. Thank you for explaining everything to me. I appreciate your help.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
The conversation has concluded. The eligibility agent has clearly communicated that I am not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because:

1. Alex is 16 years old (over the automatic qualification age of under 16)
2. He is not in approved education or training
3. The 20-week extension period after leaving education has expired (it's been 6-7 months)

The agent also helpfully explained that if Alex returns to approved education or training in the future, I could claim Child Benefit again at that time. The eligibility determination has been made, so I've invoked the exit_loop function as instructed.
